description: A schematic diagram of a near-field terahertz (THz) imaging setup showing a curved (S-shaped) waveguide or channel (yellow) connecting a THz source on the left to a detector on the right, with a localized near-field probe or interaction region (blue ellipse) positioned at the center of the structure.
key_findings: Near-field probing is performed at a localized region within a guided THz structure
The geometry suggests confinement and controlled propagation of THz radiation through a curved path
suggested_knowledge_points: Principles of near-field THz imaging and how it surpasses diffraction limits
Waveguide-based confinement and manipulation of THz radiation
Role of localized probes in detecting evanescent fields
